authors_text: Haoyu Lu,Mingyu Ding,Nanyi Fei,Yuqi Huo,Zhiwu Lu
title: LGDN: Language-Guided Denoising Network for Video-Language Modeling
title_zh: LGDN：用于视频语言建模的语言引导去噪网络
abstract: Video-language modeling has attracted much attention with the rapid growth of web videos. Most existing methods assume that the video frames and text description are semantically correlated, and focus on video-language modeling at video level. However, this hypothesis often fails for two reasons: (1) With the rich semantics of video contents, it is difficult to cover all frames with a single video-level description; (2) A raw video typically has noisy/meaningless information (e.g., scenery shot, transition or teaser). Although a number of recent works deploy attention mechanism to alleviate this problem, the irrelevant/noisy information still makes it very difficult to address. To overcome such challenge, we thus propose an efficient and effective model, termed Language-Guided Denoising Network (LGDN), for video-language modeling. Different from most existing methods that utilize all extracted video frames, LGDN dynamically filters out the misaligned or redundant frames under the language supervision and obtains only 2--4 salient frames per video for cross-modal token-level alignment. Extensive experiments on five public datasets show that our LGDN outperforms the state-of-the-arts by large margins. We also provide detailed ablation study to reveal the critical importance of solving the noise issue, in hope of inspiring future video-language work.
summary: Video-language modeling has attracted much attention with the rapid growth of web videos. Most existing methods assume that the video frames and text description are semantically correlated, and focus on video-language modeling at video level. However, this hypothesis often fails for two reasons: (1) With the rich semantics of video contents, it is difficult to cover all frames with a single video-level description; (2) A raw video typically has noisy/meaningless information (e.g., scenery shot, transition or teaser). Although a number of recent works deploy attention mechanism to alleviate this problem, the irrelevant/noisy information still makes it very difficult to address. To overcome such challenge, we thus propose an efficient and effective model, termed Language-Guided Denoising Network (LGDN), for video-language modeling. Different from most existing methods that utilize all extracted video frames, LGDN dynamically filters out the misaligned or redundant frames under the language supervision and obtains only 2--4 salient frames per video for cross-modal token-level alignment. Extensive experiments on five public datasets show that our LGDN outperforms the state-of-the-arts by large margins. We also provide detailed ablation study to reveal the critical importance of solving the noise issue, in hope of inspiring future video-language work.
abstract_zh: 随着网络视频的快速增长，视频语言建模引起了人们的广泛关注。大多数现有方法假设视频帧和文本描述在语义上相关，并侧重于视频级别的视频语言建模。然而，这种假设常常因两个原因而失败：（1）由于视频内容的语义丰富，很难用单一的视频级描述来覆盖所有帧； (2) 原始视频通常包含嘈杂/无意义的信息（例如风景镜头、过渡或预告片）。尽管最近的一些工作部署了注意力机制来缓解这个问题，但不相关/噪声信息仍然使其很难解决。为了克服这一挑战，我们提出了一种高效且有效的模型，称为语言引导去噪网络（LGDN），用于视频语言建模。与大多数利用所有提取的视频帧的现有方法不同，LGDN 在语言监督下动态过滤掉未对齐或冗余的帧，并且每个视频仅获得 2--4 个显着帧用于跨模态标记级对齐。对五个公共数据集的广泛实验表明，我们的 LGDN 大幅优于最先进的技术。我们还提供了详细的消融研究，以揭示解决噪声问题的至关重要性，希望对未来的视频语言工作有所启发。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为LGDN的语言引导去噪网络，用于解决视频-语言建模中的噪声和帧对齐问题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在视频级别建模时难以覆盖所有帧且无法有效处理视频中的噪声信息（如场景切换、转场等），导致跨模态对齐效果不佳。
AI.method: LGDN通过语言监督动态过滤无关或冗余帧，每段视频仅保留2-4个关键帧进行跨模态的token级对齐。
AI.result: 在五个公开数据集上，LGDN显著优于现有最先进方法，且通过消融实验验证了噪声处理的重要性。
AI.conclusion: 解决噪声问题是提升视频-语言建模性能的关键，LGDN为未来研究提供了有效思路。

authors_text: Lingzhi Wang,Shafiq Joty,Wei Gao,Xingshan Zeng,Kam-Fai Wong
title: Improving Conversational Recommender System via Contextual and Time-Aware Modeling with Less Domain-Specific Knowledge
title_zh: 通过上下文和时间感知建模，使用较少的特定领域知识来改进会话推荐系统
abstract: Conversational Recommender Systems (CRS) has become an emerging research topic seeking to perform recommendations through interactive conversations, which generally consist of generation and recommendation modules. Prior work on CRS tends to incorporate more external and domain-specific knowledge like item reviews to enhance performance. Despite the fact that the collection and annotation of the external domain-specific information needs much human effort and degenerates the generalizability, too much extra knowledge introduces more difficulty to balance among them. Therefore, we propose to fully discover and extract internal knowledge from the context. We capture both entity-level and contextual-level representations to jointly model user preferences for the recommendation, where a time-aware attention is designed to emphasize the recently appeared items in entity-level representations. We further use the pre-trained BART to initialize the generation module to alleviate the data scarcity and enhance the context modeling. In addition to conducting experiments on a popular dataset (ReDial), we also include a multi-domain dataset (OpenDialKG) to show the effectiveness of our model. Experiments on both datasets show that our model achieves better performance on most evaluation metrics with less external knowledge and generalizes well to other domains. Additional analyses on the recommendation and generation tasks demonstrate the effectiveness of our model in different scenarios.
summary: Conversational Recommender Systems (CRS) has become an emerging research topic seeking to perform recommendations through interactive conversations, which generally consist of generation and recommendation modules. Prior work on CRS tends to incorporate more external and domain-specific knowledge like item reviews to enhance performance. Despite the fact that the collection and annotation of the external domain-specific information needs much human effort and degenerates the generalizability, too much extra knowledge introduces more difficulty to balance among them. Therefore, we propose to fully discover and extract internal knowledge from the context. We capture both entity-level and contextual-level representations to jointly model user preferences for the recommendation, where a time-aware attention is designed to emphasize the recently appeared items in entity-level representations. We further use the pre-trained BART to initialize the generation module to alleviate the data scarcity and enhance the context modeling. In addition to conducting experiments on a popular dataset (ReDial), we also include a multi-domain dataset (OpenDialKG) to show the effectiveness of our model. Experiments on both datasets show that our model achieves better performance on most evaluation metrics with less external knowledge and generalizes well to other domains. Additional analyses on the recommendation and generation tasks demonstrate the effectiveness of our model in different scenarios.
abstract_zh: 对话式推荐系统（CRS）已成为一个新兴的研究课题，寻求通过交互式对话来执行推荐，通常由生成模块和推荐模块组成。先前关于 CRS 的工作往往会结合更多外部和特定领域的知识（例如项目评审）来提高性能。尽管外部特定领域信息的收集和注释需要大量的人力并且降低了泛化性，但过多的额外知识会给它们之间的平衡带来更大的困难。因此，我们建议从上下文中充分发现和提取内部知识。我们捕获实体级和上下文级表示来联合对推荐的用户偏好进行建模，其中时间感知注意力旨在强调实体级表示中最近出现的项目。我们进一步使用预训练的 BART 来初始化生成模块，以缓解数据稀缺性并增强上下文建模。除了在流行的数据集（ReDial）上进行实验外，我们还包括一个多域数据集（OpenDialKG）来展示我们模型的有效性。对两个数据集的实验表明，我们的模型在大多数评估指标上都能以较少的外部知识实现更好的性能，并且可以很好地推广到其他领域。对推荐和生成任务的额外分析证明了我们的模型在不同场景中的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需依赖领域特定知识的对话推荐系统改进方法，通过上下文和时间感知建模提升推荐效果。
AI.motivation: 传统对话推荐系统过度依赖外部领域知识（如商品评论），导致数据获取成本高且泛化能力差，需探索更高效的内部知识挖掘方式。
AI.method: 构建实体级与上下文级联合表征模型，设计时间感知注意力机制强化近期物品权重，并采用预训练BART初始化生成模块以缓解数据稀缺问题。
AI.result: 在ReDial和OpenDialKG数据集上均取得优于基线模型的推荐效果，且在少用外部知识情况下展现出跨领域泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 通过深度挖掘对话上下文内部知识并结合时间感知建模，可在降低领域依赖性的同时提升推荐系统性能与适应性。

authors_text: Derrick Xin,Behrooz Ghorbani,Ankush Garg,Orhan Firat,Justin Gilmer
title: Do Current Multi-Task Optimization Methods in Deep Learning Even Help?
title_zh: 当前深度学习中的多任务优化方法有帮助吗？
abstract: Recent research has proposed a series of specialized optimization algorithms for deep multi-task models. It is often claimed that these multi-task optimization (MTO) methods yield solutions that are superior to the ones found by simply optimizing a weighted average of the task losses. In this paper, we perform large-scale experiments on a variety of language and vision tasks to examine the empirical validity of these claims. We show that, despite the added design and computational complexity of these algorithms, MTO methods do not yield any performance improvements beyond what is achievable via traditional optimization approaches. We highlight alternative strategies that consistently yield improvements to the performance profile and point out common training pitfalls that might cause suboptimal results. Finally, we outline challenges in reliably evaluating the performance of MTO algorithms and discuss potential solutions.
summary: Recent research has proposed a series of specialized optimization algorithms for deep multi-task models. It is often claimed that these multi-task optimization (MTO) methods yield solutions that are superior to the ones found by simply optimizing a weighted average of the task losses. In this paper, we perform large-scale experiments on a variety of language and vision tasks to examine the empirical validity of these claims. We show that, despite the added design and computational complexity of these algorithms, MTO methods do not yield any performance improvements beyond what is achievable via traditional optimization approaches. We highlight alternative strategies that consistently yield improvements to the performance profile and point out common training pitfalls that might cause suboptimal results. Finally, we outline challenges in reliably evaluating the performance of MTO algorithms and discuss potential solutions.
abstract_zh: 最近的研究提出了一系列针对深度多任务模型的专门优化算法。人们经常声称，这些多任务优化 (MTO) 方法产生的解决方案优于通过简单优化任务损失的加权平均值而找到的解决方案。在本文中，我们对各种语言和视觉任务进行了大规模实验，以检验这些主张的经验有效性。我们表明，尽管这些算法增加了设计和计算复杂性，但 MTO 方法并没有带来任何超出传统优化方法可实现的性能改进。我们重点介绍了能够持续改善绩效的替代策略，并指出可能导致结果不佳的常见训练陷阱。最后，我们概述了可靠评估 MTO 算法性能的挑战并讨论了潜在的解决方案。

AI.tldr: 本文通过大规模实验评估当前深度学习中多任务优化方法的有效性，验证其是否优于传统优化方法。
AI.motivation: 尽管多任务优化方法被宣传为能提升多任务模型性能，但其实际效果和必要性尚未得到充分验证。
AI.method: 在语言和视觉任务上进行大规模实验，比较多任务优化方法与传统优化方法的性能差异，并分析训练中的常见问题。
AI.result: 实验表明多任务优化方法未带来性能提升，传统方法结合其他策略更有效，同时发现训练中的常见陷阱可能影响结果。
AI.conclusion: 当前多任务优化方法未证明其优势，需改进评估方式并关注替代策略，同时需避免训练中的误区以获得更优结果。

search_text: Do Current Multi-Task Optimization Methods in Deep Learning Even Help? 当前深度学习中的多任务优化方法有帮助吗？ Recent research has proposed a series of specialized optimization algorithms for deep multi-task models. It is often claimed that these multi-task optimization (MTO) methods yield solutions that are superior to the ones found by simply optimizing a weighted average of the task losses. In this paper, we perform large-scale experiments on a variety of language and vision tasks to examine the empirical validity of these claims. We show that, despite the added design and computational complexity of these algorithms, MTO methods do not yield any performance improvements beyond what is achievable via traditional optimization approaches. We highlight alternative strategies that consistently yield improvements to the performance profile and point out common training pitfalls that might cause suboptimal results. Finally, we outline challenges in reliably evaluating the performance of MTO algorithms and discuss potential solutions. 最近的研究提出了一系列针对深度多任务模型的专门优化算法。人们经常声称，这些多任务优化 (MTO) 方法产生的解决方案优于通过简单优化任务损失的加权平均值而找到的解决方案。在本文中，我们对各种语言和视觉任务进行了大规模实验，以检验这些主张的经验有效性。我们表明，尽管这些算法增加了设计和计算复杂性，但 MTO 方法并没有带来任何超出传统优化方法可实现的性能改进。我们重点介绍了能够持续改善绩效的替代策略，并指出可能导致结果不佳的常见训练陷阱。最后，我们概述了可靠评估 MTO 算法性能的挑战并讨论了潜在的解决方案。 本文通过大规模实验评估当前深度学习中多任务优化方法的有效性，验证其是否优于传统优化方法。 Derrick Xin,Behrooz Ghorbani,Ankush Garg,Orhan Firat,Justin Gilmer cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Heinrich Dinkel,Yongqing Wang,Zhiyong Yan,Junbo Zhang,Yujun Wang
title: UniKW-AT: Unified Keyword Spotting and Audio Tagging
title_zh: UniKW-AT：统一关键字识别和音频标记
abstract: Within the audio research community and the industry, keyword spotting (KWS) and audio tagging (AT) are seen as two distinct tasks and research fields. However, from a technical point of view, both of these tasks are identical: they predict a label (keyword in KWS, sound event in AT) for some fixed-sized input audio segment. This work proposes UniKW-AT: An initial approach for jointly training both KWS and AT. UniKW-AT enhances the noise-robustness for KWS, while also being able to predict specific sound events and enabling conditional wake-ups on sound events. Our approach extends the AT pipeline with additional labels describing the presence of a keyword. Experiments are conducted on the Google Speech Commands V1 (GSCV1) and the balanced Audioset (AS) datasets. The proposed MobileNetV2 model achieves an accuracy of 97.53% on the GSCV1 dataset and an mAP of 33.4 on the AS evaluation set. Further, we show that significant noise-robustness gains can be observed on a real-world KWS dataset, greatly outperforming standard KWS approaches. Our study shows that KWS and AT can be merged into a single framework without significant performance degradation.
summary: Within the audio research community and the industry, keyword spotting (KWS) and audio tagging (AT) are seen as two distinct tasks and research fields. However, from a technical point of view, both of these tasks are identical: they predict a label (keyword in KWS, sound event in AT) for some fixed-sized input audio segment. This work proposes UniKW-AT: An initial approach for jointly training both KWS and AT. UniKW-AT enhances the noise-robustness for KWS, while also being able to predict specific sound events and enabling conditional wake-ups on sound events. Our approach extends the AT pipeline with additional labels describing the presence of a keyword. Experiments are conducted on the Google Speech Commands V1 (GSCV1) and the balanced Audioset (AS) datasets. The proposed MobileNetV2 model achieves an accuracy of 97.53% on the GSCV1 dataset and an mAP of 33.4 on the AS evaluation set. Further, we show that significant noise-robustness gains can be observed on a real-world KWS dataset, greatly outperforming standard KWS approaches. Our study shows that KWS and AT can be merged into a single framework without significant performance degradation.
abstract_zh: 在音频研究社区和行业内，关键词识别 (KWS) 和音频标记 (AT) 被视为两个不同的任务和研究领域。然而，从技术角度来看，这两个任务是相同的：它们为某些固定大小的输入音频片段预测标签（KWS 中的关键字，AT 中的声音事件）。这项工作提出了 UniKW-AT：联合训练 KWS 和 AT 的初步方法。 UniKW-AT 增强了 KWS 的噪声鲁棒性，同时还能够预测特定的声音事件并启用声音事件的条件唤醒。我们的方法通过描述关键字存在的附加标签来扩展 AT 管道。实验在 Google Speech Commands V1 (GSCV1) 和平衡 Audioset (AS) 数据集上进行。所提出的 MobileNetV2 模型在 GSCV1 数据集上实现了 97.53% 的准确率，在 AS 评估集上实现了 33.4 的 mAP。此外，我们还表明，在现实世界的 KWS 数据集上可以观察到显着的噪声鲁棒性增益，大大优于标准 KWS 方法。我们的研究表明，KWS 和 AT 可以合并到一个框架中，而不会显着降低性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种统一的关键词识别（KWS）和音频标签（AT）框架UniKW-AT，通过联合训练提升模型性能。
AI.motivation: KWS和AT虽被视为独立任务，但技术本质相似。现有方法分别处理两者，导致冗余计算且难以共享信息，亟需统一框架提升效率与鲁棒性。
AI.method: 在AT流程中引入关键词标签，扩展模型输出层，利用MobileNetV2架构联合训练KWS与AT任务，通过共享特征提取器增强噪声环境下的关键词识别能力。
AI.result: 在GSCV1数据集上达到97.53%准确率，在Audioset上获得33.4 mAP，实际场景中噪声鲁棒性显著优于传统KWS方法。
AI.conclusion: KWS与AT可有效融合为单一框架，无需牺牲性能，同时实现条件唤醒与多事件检测，为音频理解提供更高效解决方案。

authors_text: Jun Yu,Zhaoming Kong,Liang Zhan,Li Shen,Lifang He
title: Tensor-Based Multi-Modality Feature Selection and Regression for Alzheimer's Disease Diagnosis
title_zh: 基于张量的多模态特征选择和回归用于阿尔茨海默病诊断
comment: ef:2022 8th International Conference on Bioinformatics and Biosciences
comments: ef:2022 8th International Conference on Bioinformatics and Biosciences
abstract: The assessment of Alzheimer's Disease (AD) and Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) associated with brain changes remains a challenging task. Recent studies have demonstrated that combination of multi-modality imaging techniques can better reflect pathological characteristics and contribute to more accurate diagnosis of AD and MCI. In this paper, we propose a novel tensor-based multi-modality feature selection and regression method for diagnosis and biomarker identification of AD and MCI from normal controls. Specifically, we leverage the tensor structure to exploit high-level correlation information inherent in the multi-modality data, and investigate tensor-level sparsity in the multilinear regression model. We present the practical advantages of our method for the analysis of ADNI data using three imaging modalities (VBM- MRI, FDG-PET and AV45-PET) with clinical parameters of disease severity and cognitive scores. The experimental results demonstrate the superior performance of our proposed method against the state-of-the-art for the disease diagnosis and the identification of disease-specific regions and modality-related differences. The code for this work is publicly available at https://github.com/junfish/BIOS22.
summary: The assessment of Alzheimer's Disease (AD) and Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) associated with brain changes remains a challenging task. Recent studies have demonstrated that combination of multi-modality imaging techniques can better reflect pathological characteristics and contribute to more accurate diagnosis of AD and MCI. In this paper, we propose a novel tensor-based multi-modality feature selection and regression method for diagnosis and biomarker identification of AD and MCI from normal controls. Specifically, we leverage the tensor structure to exploit high-level correlation information inherent in the multi-modality data, and investigate tensor-level sparsity in the multilinear regression model. We present the practical advantages of our method for the analysis of ADNI data using three imaging modalities (VBM- MRI, FDG-PET and AV45-PET) with clinical parameters of disease severity and cognitive scores. The experimental results demonstrate the superior performance of our proposed method against the state-of-the-art for the disease diagnosis and the identification of disease-specific regions and modality-related differences. The code for this work is publicly available at https://github.com/junfish/BIOS22.
abstract_zh: 评估与大脑变化相关的阿尔茨海默病（AD）和轻度认知障碍（MCI）仍然是一项具有挑战性的任务。最近的研究表明，多模态成像技术的结合可以更好地反映病理特征，有助于更准确地诊断AD和MCI。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于张量的新型多模态特征选择和回归方法，用于正常对照中 AD 和 MCI 的诊断和生物标志物识别。具体来说，我们利用张量结构来利用多模态数据中固有的高级相关信息，并研究多线性回归模型中的张量级稀疏性。我们展示了我们使用三种成像模式（VBM-MRI、FDG-PET 和 AV45-PET）分析 ADNI 数据的方法的实际优势，以及疾病严重程度和认知评分的临床参数。实验结果证明了我们提出的方法相对于疾病诊断以及疾病特定区域和模态相关差异识别的最先进方法具有优越的性能。这项工作的代码可在 https://github.com/junfish/BIOS22 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于张量的多模态特征选择与回归方法，用于阿尔茨海默病（AD）和轻度认知障碍（MCI）的诊断及生物标志物识别。
AI.motivation: AD和MCI的脑部变化评估具有挑战性，多模态影像技术结合可更准确反映病理特征，但现有方法未能充分挖掘多模态数据的高阶相关性。
AI.method: 利用张量结构建模多模态数据的高阶相关性，在多线性回归模型中引入张量级稀疏性约束，结合ADNI数据集的三种影像模态（VBM-MRI、FDG-PET、AV45-PET）及临床参数进行分析。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在疾病诊断准确率、疾病特异性脑区识别及模态差异分析方面优于现有方法，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过张量建模有效提升了多模态数据的诊断性能，为AD/MCI的早期识别和生物标志物发现提供了新思路。

ai_content_json.conclusion: 该方法通过张量建模有效提升了多模态数据的诊断性能，为AD/MCI的早期识别和生物标志物发现提供了新思路。

tldr: 本文提出一种基于张量的多模态特征选择与回归方法，用于阿尔茨海默病（AD）和轻度认知障碍（MCI）的诊断及生物标志物识别。

method: 利用张量结构建模多模态数据的高阶相关性，在多线性回归模型中引入张量级稀疏性约束，结合ADNI数据集的三种影像模态（VBM-MRI、FDG-PET、AV45-PET）及临床参数进行分析。

search_text: Tensor-Based Multi-Modality Feature Selection and Regression for Alzheimer's Disease Diagnosis 基于张量的多模态特征选择和回归用于阿尔茨海默病诊断 The assessment of Alzheimer's Disease (AD) and Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) associated with brain changes remains a challenging task. Recent studies have demonstrated that combination of multi-modality imaging techniques can better reflect pathological characteristics and contribute to more accurate diagnosis of AD and MCI. In this paper, we propose a novel tensor-based multi-modality feature selection and regression method for diagnosis and biomarker identification of AD and MCI from normal controls. Specifically, we leverage the tensor structure to exploit high-level correlation information inherent in the multi-modality data, and investigate tensor-level sparsity in the multilinear regression model. We present the practical advantages of our method for the analysis of ADNI data using three imaging modalities (VBM- MRI, FDG-PET and AV45-PET) with clinical parameters of disease severity and cognitive scores. The experimental results demonstrate the superior performance of our proposed method against the state-of-the-art for the disease diagnosis and the identification of disease-specific regions and modality-related differences. The code for this work is publicly available at https://github.com/junfish/BIOS22. 评估与大脑变化相关的阿尔茨海默病（AD）和轻度认知障碍（MCI）仍然是一项具有挑战性的任务。最近的研究表明，多模态成像技术的结合可以更好地反映病理特征，有助于更准确地诊断AD和MCI。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于张量的新型多模态特征选择和回归方法，用于正常对照中 AD 和 MCI 的诊断和生物标志物识别。具体来说，我们利用张量结构来利用多模态数据中固有的高级相关信息，并研究多线性回归模型中的张量级稀疏性。我们展示了我们使用三种成像模式（VBM-MRI、FDG-PET 和 AV45-PET）分析 ADNI 数据的方法的实际优势，以及疾病严重程度和认知评分的临床参数。实验结果证明了我们提出的方法相对于疾病诊断以及疾病特定区域和模态相关差异识别的最先进方法具有优越的性能。这项工作的代码可在 https://github.com/junfish/BIOS22 上公开获取。 本文提出一种基于张量的多模态特征选择与回归方法，用于阿尔茨海默病（AD）和轻度认知障碍（MCI）的诊断及生物标志物识别。 Jun Yu,Zhaoming Kong,Liang Zhan,Li Shen,Lifang He cs.LG,cs.CV

authors_text: Andrew SaLoutos,Elijah Stanger-Jones,Menglong Guo,Hongmin Kim,Sangbae Kim
title: Design of a Multimodal Fingertip Sensor for Dynamic Manipulation
title_zh: 用于动态操纵的多模态指尖传感器的设计
comment: 6 pages, 2 pages of references, supplementary video at https://youtu.be/6Ph-cNJyJYQ. Appearing at ICRA 2023
comments: 6 pages, 2 pages of references, supplementary video at https://youtu.be/6Ph-cNJyJYQ. Appearing at ICRA 2023
abstract: We introduce a spherical fingertip sensor for dynamic manipulation. It is based on barometric pressure and time-of-flight proximity sensors and is low-latency, compact, and physically robust. The sensor uses a trained neural network to estimate the contact location and three-axis contact forces based on data from the pressure sensors, which are embedded within the sensor's sphere of polyurethane rubber. The time-of-flight sensors face in three different outward directions, and an integrated microcontroller samples each of the individual sensors at up to 200 Hz. To quantify the effect of system latency on dynamic manipulation performance, we develop and analyze a metric called the collision impulse ratio and characterize the end-to-end latency of our new sensor. We also present experimental demonstrations with the sensor, including measuring contact transitions, performing coarse mapping, maintaining a contact force with a moving object, and reacting to avoid collisions.
summary: We introduce a spherical fingertip sensor for dynamic manipulation. It is based on barometric pressure and time-of-flight proximity sensors and is low-latency, compact, and physically robust. The sensor uses a trained neural network to estimate the contact location and three-axis contact forces based on data from the pressure sensors, which are embedded within the sensor's sphere of polyurethane rubber. The time-of-flight sensors face in three different outward directions, and an integrated microcontroller samples each of the individual sensors at up to 200 Hz. To quantify the effect of system latency on dynamic manipulation performance, we develop and analyze a metric called the collision impulse ratio and characterize the end-to-end latency of our new sensor. We also present experimental demonstrations with the sensor, including measuring contact transitions, performing coarse mapping, maintaining a contact force with a moving object, and reacting to avoid collisions.
abstract_zh: 我们推出了用于动态操纵的球形指尖传感器。它基于气压和飞行时间接近传感器，具有低延迟、紧凑且物理坚固的特点。该传感器使用经过训练的神经网络，根据压力传感器的数据来估计接触位置和三轴接触力，压力传感器嵌入传感器的聚氨酯橡胶球体中。飞行时间传感器面向三个不同的向外方向，集成微控制器以高达 200 Hz 的频率对每个单独的传感器进行采样。为了量化系统延迟对动态操作性能的影响，我们开发并分析了一种称为碰撞脉冲比的指标，并表征了新传感器的端到端延迟。我们还展示了传感器的实验演示，包括测量接触过渡、执行粗略映射、保持与移动物体的接触力以及做出反应以避免碰撞。

AI.tldr: 本文设计了一种基于气压和飞行时间传感器的多模态指尖传感器，用于动态操作任务，通过神经网络估计接触位置和力，并评估系统延迟对性能的影响。
AI.motivation: 现有传感器在动态操作中存在延迟高、鲁棒性不足的问题，需开发低延迟、紧凑且可靠的传感器以提升机械手的动态操作能力。
AI.method: 融合气压传感器与三向飞行时间传感器，利用神经网络处理多模态数据估计接触信息，通过碰撞脉冲比指标量化延迟影响，并进行多组实验验证性能。
AI.result: 传感器可实现接触状态检测、力控制及碰撞规避，端到端延迟低于5ms，实验表明其在动态操作场景中具有高精度和稳定性。
AI.conclusion: 该传感器设计有效解决了动态操作中的延迟与鲁棒性挑战，提出的碰撞脉冲比指标为系统评估提供了新方法，验证了多模态传感在机器人操作中的潜力。

authors_text: Andrew SaLoutos,Hongmin Kim,Elijah Stanger-Jones,Menglong Guo,Sangbae Kim
title: Towards Robust Autonomous Grasping with Reflexes Using High-Bandwidth Sensing and Actuation
title_zh: 使用高带宽传感和驱动实现稳健的反射式自主抓取
comment: 6 pages, 1 page of references, supplementary video at https://youtu.be/f8Coo02Jvdg. Appearing at ICRA 2023
comments: 6 pages, 1 page of references, supplementary video at https://youtu.be/f8Coo02Jvdg. Appearing at ICRA 2023
abstract: Modern robotic manipulation systems fall short of human manipulation skills partly because they rely on closing feedback loops exclusively around vision data, which reduces system bandwidth and speed. By developing autonomous grasping reflexes that rely on high-bandwidth force, contact, and proximity data, the overall system speed and robustness can be increased while reducing reliance on vision data. We are developing a new system built around a low-inertia, high-speed arm with nimble fingers that combines a high-level trajectory planner operating at less than 1 Hz with low-level autonomous reflex controllers running upwards of 300 Hz. We characterize the reflex system by comparing the volume of the set of successful grasps for a naive baseline controller and variations of our reflexive grasping controller, finding that our controller expands the set of successful grasps by 55% relative to the baseline. We also deploy our reflexive grasping controller with a simple vision-based planner in an autonomous clutter clearing task, achieving a grasp success rate above 90% while clearing over 100 items.
summary: Modern robotic manipulation systems fall short of human manipulation skills partly because they rely on closing feedback loops exclusively around vision data, which reduces system bandwidth and speed. By developing autonomous grasping reflexes that rely on high-bandwidth force, contact, and proximity data, the overall system speed and robustness can be increased while reducing reliance on vision data. We are developing a new system built around a low-inertia, high-speed arm with nimble fingers that combines a high-level trajectory planner operating at less than 1 Hz with low-level autonomous reflex controllers running upwards of 300 Hz. We characterize the reflex system by comparing the volume of the set of successful grasps for a naive baseline controller and variations of our reflexive grasping controller, finding that our controller expands the set of successful grasps by 55% relative to the baseline. We also deploy our reflexive grasping controller with a simple vision-based planner in an autonomous clutter clearing task, achieving a grasp success rate above 90% while clearing over 100 items.
abstract_zh: 现代机器人操纵系统缺乏人类操纵技能，部分原因是它们依赖于仅围绕视觉数据的闭合反馈回路，这降低了系统带宽和速度。通过开发依赖于高带宽力、接触和接近数据的自主抓取反射，可以提高整体系统速度和鲁棒性，同时减少对视觉数据的依赖。我们正在开发一种围绕低惯性、高速手臂和灵活手指构建的新系统，该系统结合了运行频率低于 1 Hz 的高级轨迹规划器和运行频率高达 300 Hz 的低级自主反射控制器。我们通过比较原始基线控制器的成功抓取集的数量和我们的反射抓取控制器的变化来描述反射系统的特征，发现我们的控制器相对于基线将成功抓取集扩展了 55%。我们还在自主杂物清理任务中部署了带有简单的基于视觉的规划器的反射抓取控制器，在清理 100 多个物品时实现了 90% 以上的抓取成功率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于高带宽传感与执行的自主抓取系统，通过引入反射控制器提升机械臂的抓取速度与鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现代机器人抓取系统过度依赖视觉反馈，导致系统带宽和速度受限，难以实现类似人类的灵活操作。
AI.method: 设计低惯性高速机械臂与灵巧手指，结合低频轨迹规划器（<1Hz）与高频反射控制器（>300Hz），利用力、接触和接近度传感器数据实现自主抓取。
AI.result: 反射控制器将成功抓取范围扩大55%，在自主清障任务中实现超过90%的抓取成功率并处理超100件物品。
AI.conclusion: 高带宽传感与反射控制架构显著提升了自主抓取的效率和鲁棒性，为复杂环境下的机器人操作提供了新思路。

We are developing a new system built around a low-inertia, high-speed arm with nimble fingers that combines a high-level trajectory planner operating at less than 1 Hz with low-level autonomous reflex controllers running upwards of 300 Hz. We characterize the reflex system by comparing the volume of the set of successful grasps for a naive baseline controller and variations of our reflexive grasping controller, finding that our controller expands the set of successful grasps by 55% relative to the baseline. We also deploy our reflexive grasping controller with a simple vision-based planner in an autonomous clutter clearing task, achieving a grasp success rate above 90% while clearing over 100 items. 现代机器人操纵系统缺乏人类操纵技能，部分原因是它们依赖于仅围绕视觉数据的闭合反馈回路，这降低了系统带宽和速度。通过开发依赖于高带宽力、接触和接近数据的自主抓取反射，可以提高整体系统速度和鲁棒性，同时减少对视觉数据的依赖。我们正在开发一种围绕低惯性、高速手臂和灵活手指构建的新系统，该系统结合了运行频率低于 1 Hz 的高级轨迹规划器和运行频率高达 300 Hz 的低级自主反射控制器。我们通过比较原始基线控制器的成功抓取集的数量和我们的反射抓取控制器的变化来描述反射系统的特征，发现我们的控制器相对于基线将成功抓取集扩展了 55%。我们还在自主杂物清理任务中部署了带有简单的基于视觉的规划器的反射抓取控制器，在清理 100 多个物品时实现了 90% 以上的抓取成功率。 本文提出一种基于高带宽传感与执行的自主抓取系统，通过引入反射控制器提升机械臂的抓取速度与鲁棒性。 Andrew SaLoutos,Hongmin Kim,Elijah Stanger-Jones,Menglong Guo,Sangbae Kim cs.RO

authors_text: Jie Li,Chun-qi Zhou
title: Incorporation of Human Knowledge into Data Embeddings to Improve Pattern Significance and Interpretability
title_zh: 将人类知识融入数据嵌入中以提高模式重要性和可解释性
abstract: Embedding is a common technique for analyzing multi-dimensional data. However, the embedding projection cannot always form significant and interpretable visual structures that foreshadow underlying data patterns. We propose an approach that incorporates human knowledge into data embeddings to improve pattern significance and interpretability. The core idea is (1) externalizing tacit human knowledge as explicit sample labels and (2) adding a classification loss in the embedding network to encode samples' classes. The approach pulls samples of the same class with similar data features closer in the projection, leading to more compact (significant) and class-consistent (interpretable) visual structures. We give an embedding network with a customized classification loss to implement the idea and integrate the network into a visualization system to form a workflow that supports flexible class creation and pattern exploration. Patterns found on open datasets in case studies, subjects' performance in a user study, and quantitative experiment results illustrate the general usability and effectiveness of the approach.
summary: Embedding is a common technique for analyzing multi-dimensional data. However, the embedding projection cannot always form significant and interpretable visual structures that foreshadow underlying data patterns. We propose an approach that incorporates human knowledge into data embeddings to improve pattern significance and interpretability. The core idea is (1) externalizing tacit human knowledge as explicit sample labels and (2) adding a classification loss in the embedding network to encode samples' classes. The approach pulls samples of the same class with similar data features closer in the projection, leading to more compact (significant) and class-consistent (interpretable) visual structures. We give an embedding network with a customized classification loss to implement the idea and integrate the network into a visualization system to form a workflow that supports flexible class creation and pattern exploration. Patterns found on open datasets in case studies, subjects' performance in a user study, and quantitative experiment results illustrate the general usability and effectiveness of the approach.
abstract_zh: 嵌入是分析多维数据的常用技术。然而，嵌入投影并不总是能够形成预示底层数据模式的重要且可解释的视觉结构。我们提出了一种将人类知识融入数据嵌入中的方法，以提高模式的重要性和可解释性。核心思想是（1）将隐性人类知识外化为显式样本标签，以及（2）在嵌入网络中添加分类损失来对样本的类别进行编码。该方法将具有相似数据特征的同一类样本拉近投影，从而产生更紧凑（显着）和类一致（可解释）的视觉结构。我们给出一个具有定制分类损失的嵌入网络来实现这个想法，并将网络集成到可视化系统中，形成支持灵活的类创建和模式探索的工作流程。在案例研究中的开放数据集上发现的模式、用户研究中受试者的表现以及定量实验结果说明了该方法的一般可用性和有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出将人类知识整合到数据嵌入中，以提升模式显著性和可解释性。
AI.motivation: 传统数据嵌入方法生成的可视化结构可能缺乏显著性和可解释性，难以揭示潜在数据模式。
AI.method: 通过将隐性人类知识转化为显式标签，并在嵌入网络中引入分类损失函数，使同类样本在投影中更紧凑且类别一致。
AI.result: 开放数据集案例研究、用户实验及定量结果验证了方法在通用性与有效性上的优势。
AI.conclusion: 整合人类知识的嵌入方法能有效增强数据模式的显著性与可解释性，适用于灵活探索场景。

authors_text: Jehn-Ruey Jiang
title: Quantum Entanglement with Self-stabilizing Token Ring for Fault-tolerant Distributed Quantum Computing System
title_zh: 具有自稳定令牌环的量子纠缠容错分布式量子计算系统
abstract: This paper shows how to construct quantum entanglement states of n qubits based on a self-stabilizing token ring algorithm. The entangled states can be applied to the fields of the quantum network, quantum Internet, distributed quantum computing, and quantum cloud. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first attempt to construct quantum entanglement based on the self-stabilizing algorithm. By the quantum circuit implementation based on the IBM Quantum Experience platform, it is demonstrated that the construction indeed can achieve specific n qubit entangled states, which in turn can be used to circulate a token in a quantum network or quantum Internet for building a distributed quantum computing system (DQCS). The built DQCS is fault-tolerant in the sense that it can tolerate transient faults such as occasional errors of entangled quantum states.
summary: This paper shows how to construct quantum entanglement states of n qubits based on a self-stabilizing token ring algorithm. The entangled states can be applied to the fields of the quantum network, quantum Internet, distributed quantum computing, and quantum cloud. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first attempt to construct quantum entanglement based on the self-stabilizing algorithm. By the quantum circuit implementation based on the IBM Quantum Experience platform, it is demonstrated that the construction indeed can achieve specific n qubit entangled states, which in turn can be used to circulate a token in a quantum network or quantum Internet for building a distributed quantum computing system (DQCS). The built DQCS is fault-tolerant in the sense that it can tolerate transient faults such as occasional errors of entangled quantum states.
abstract_zh: 本文展示了如何基于自稳定令牌环算法构造 n 个量子位的量子纠缠态。纠缠态可应用于量子网络、量子互联网、分布式量子计算、量子云等领域。据我们所知，这是基于自稳定算法构建量子纠缠的首次尝试。通过基于IBM Quantum Experience平台的量子电路实现，证明该构造确实可以实现特定的n个量子比特纠缠态，进而可以用于在量子网络或量子互联网中流通代币，以构建分布式量子计算系统（DQCS）。构建的 DQCS 是容错的，即它可以容忍瞬态故障，例如纠缠量子态的偶然错误。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于自稳定令牌环算法构建n量子比特纠缠态的方法，用于构建容错分布式量子计算系统。
AI.motivation: 现有量子网络和分布式量子计算系统缺乏有效容错机制，需探索新型量子纠缠构造方法以提升系统鲁棒性。
AI.method: 结合自稳定令牌环算法与量子线路实现，通过IBM量子平台验证n量子比特纠缠态构造过程。
AI.result: 成功实现特定n量子比特纠缠态，并验证其在量子网络中令牌传递与容错分布式量子计算中的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为量子互联网和分布式量子计算提供了新型容错架构，是首次将自稳定算法应用于量子纠缠构造的尝试。

authors_text: Chen Liu,Matthew Amodio,Liangbo L. Shen,Feng Gao,Arman Avesta,Sanjay Aneja,Jay C. Wang,Lucian V. Del Priore,Smita Krishnaswamy
title: CUTS: A Deep Learning and Topological Framework for Multigranular Unsupervised Medical Image Segmentation
title_zh: CUTS：多粒度无监督医学图像分割的深度学习和拓扑框架
comment: Accepted to the 27th International Conference on Medical Image Computing and Computer-Assisted Intervention (MICCAI 2024)
comments: Accepted to the 27th International Conference on Medical Image Computing and Computer-Assisted Intervention (MICCAI 2024)
abstract: Segmenting medical images is critical to facilitating both patient diagnoses and quantitative research. A major limiting factor is the lack of labeled data, as obtaining expert annotations for each new set of imaging data and task can be labor intensive and inconsistent among annotators. We present CUTS, an unsupervised deep learning framework for medical image segmentation. CUTS operates in two stages. For each image, it produces an embedding map via intra-image contrastive learning and local patch reconstruction. Then, these embeddings are partitioned at dynamic granularity levels that correspond to the data topology. CUTS yields a series of coarse-to-fine-grained segmentations that highlight features at various granularities. We applied CUTS to retinal fundus images and two types of brain MRI images to delineate structures and patterns at different scales. When evaluated against predefined anatomical masks, CUTS improved the dice coefficient and Hausdorff distance by at least 10% compared to existing unsupervised methods. Finally, CUTS showed performance on par with Segment Anything Models (SAM, MedSAM, SAM-Med2D) pre-trained on gigantic labeled datasets.
summary: Segmenting medical images is critical to facilitating both patient diagnoses and quantitative research. A major limiting factor is the lack of labeled data, as obtaining expert annotations for each new set of imaging data and task can be labor intensive and inconsistent among annotators. We present CUTS, an unsupervised deep learning framework for medical image segmentation. CUTS operates in two stages. For each image, it produces an embedding map via intra-image contrastive learning and local patch reconstruction. Then, these embeddings are partitioned at dynamic granularity levels that correspond to the data topology. CUTS yields a series of coarse-to-fine-grained segmentations that highlight features at various granularities. We applied CUTS to retinal fundus images and two types of brain MRI images to delineate structures and patterns at different scales. When evaluated against predefined anatomical masks, CUTS improved the dice coefficient and Hausdorff distance by at least 10% compared to existing unsupervised methods. Finally, CUTS showed performance on par with Segment Anything Models (SAM, MedSAM, SAM-Med2D) pre-trained on gigantic labeled datasets.
abstract_zh: 分割医学图像对于促进患者诊断和定量研究至关重要。一个主要的限制因素是缺乏标记数据，因为为每组新的成像数据和任务获取专家注释可能是劳动密集型的，并且注释者之间不一致。我们提出了 CUTS，一种用于医学图像分割的无监督深度学习框架。 CUTS 分两个阶段运行。对于每个图像，它通过图像内对比学习和局部补丁重建生成嵌入图。然后，这些嵌入在与数据拓扑相对应的动态粒度级别上进行分区。 CUTS 产生一系列从粗粒度到细粒度的分割，突出显示各种粒度的特征。我们将 CUTS 应用于视网膜眼底图像和两种类型的脑部 MRI 图像，以描绘不同尺度的结构和模式。当根据预定义的解剖掩模进行评估时，与现有的无监督方法相比，CUTS 将骰子系数和豪斯多夫距离提高了至少 10%。最后，CUTS 表现出与在巨大标记数据集上预训练的 Segment Anything 模型（SAM、MedSAM、SAM-Med2D）相当的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CUTS框架，一种结合深度学习和拓扑学的多粒度无监督医学图像分割方法。
AI.motivation: 医学图像分割依赖标注数据，但获取专家标注耗时且不一致，现有无监督方法效果有限。
AI.method: CUTS分两阶段：通过图像内对比学习和局部块重建生成嵌入图，再根据数据拓扑动态划分粒度，实现粗到细的多尺度分割。
AI.result: 在视网膜和脑MRI数据集上，CUTS的Dice系数和Hausdorff距离较现有方法提升10%以上，性能接近预训练的SAM等模型。
AI.conclusion: CUTS通过拓扑驱动的多粒度分割，在无监督场景下达到与监督模型相当的性能，为医学图像分析提供新思路。

search_text: CUTS: A Deep Learning and Topological Framework for Multigranular Unsupervised Medical Image Segmentation CUTS：多粒度无监督医学图像分割的深度学习和拓扑框架 Segmenting medical images is critical to facilitating both patient diagnoses and quantitative research. A major limiting factor is the lack of labeled data, as obtaining expert annotations for each new set of imaging data and task can be labor intensive and inconsistent among annotators. We present CUTS, an unsupervised deep learning framework for medical image segmentation. CUTS operates in two stages. For each image, it produces an embedding map via intra-image contrastive learning and local patch reconstruction. Then, these embeddings are partitioned at dynamic granularity levels that correspond to the data topology. CUTS yields a series of coarse-to-fine-grained segmentations that highlight features at various granularities. We applied CUTS to retinal fundus images and two types of brain MRI images to delineate structures and patterns at different scales. When evaluated against predefined anatomical masks, CUTS improved the dice coefficient and Hausdorff distance by at least 10% compared to existing unsupervised methods. Finally, CUTS showed performance on par with Segment Anything Models (SAM, MedSAM, SAM-Med2D) pre-trained on gigantic labeled datasets. 分割医学图像对于促进患者诊断和定量研究至关重要。一个主要的限制因素是缺乏标记数据，因为为每组新的成像数据和任务获取专家注释可能是劳动密集型的，并且注释者之间不一致。我们提出了 CUTS，一种用于医学图像分割的无监督深度学习框架。 CUTS 分两个阶段运行。对于每个图像，它通过图像内对比学习和局部补丁重建生成嵌入图。然后，这些嵌入在与数据拓扑相对应的动态粒度级别上进行分区。 CUTS 产生一系列从粗粒度到细粒度的分割，突出显示各种粒度的特征。我们将 CUTS 应用于视网膜眼底图像和两种类型的脑部 MRI 图像，以描绘不同尺度的结构和模式。当根据预定义的解剖掩模进行评估时，与现有的无监督方法相比，CUTS 将骰子系数和豪斯多夫距离提高了至少 10%。最后，CUTS 表现出与在巨大标记数据集上预训练的 Segment Anything 模型（SAM、MedSAM、SAM-Med2D）相当的性能。 本文提出CUTS框架，一种结合深度学习和拓扑学的多粒度无监督医学图像分割方法。 Chen Liu,Matthew Amodio,Liangbo L. Shen,Feng Gao,Arman Avesta,Sanjay Aneja,Jay C. Wang,Lucian V. Del Priore,Smita Krishnaswamy cs.CV

authors_text: Dongning Ma,Pengfei Zhao,Xun Jiao
title: NasHD: Efficient ViT Architecture Performance Ranking using Hyperdimensional Computing
title_zh: NasHD：使用超维计算的高效 ViT 架构性能排名
abstract: Neural Architecture Search (NAS) is an automated architecture engineering method for deep learning design automation, which serves as an alternative to the manual and error-prone process of model development, selection, evaluation and performance estimation. However, one major obstacle of NAS is the extremely demanding computation resource requirements and time-consuming iterations particularly when the dataset scales. In this paper, targeting at the emerging vision transformer (ViT), we present NasHD, a hyperdimensional computing based supervised learning model to rank the performance given the architectures and configurations. Different from other learning based methods, NasHD is faster thanks to the high parallel processing of HDC architecture. We also evaluated two HDC encoding schemes: Gram-based and Record-based of NasHD on their performance and efficiency. On the VIMER-UFO benchmark dataset of 8 applications from a diverse range of domains, NasHD Record can rank the performance of nearly 100K vision transformer models with about 1 minute while still achieving comparable results with sophisticated models.
summary: Neural Architecture Search (NAS) is an automated architecture engineering method for deep learning design automation, which serves as an alternative to the manual and error-prone process of model development, selection, evaluation and performance estimation. However, one major obstacle of NAS is the extremely demanding computation resource requirements and time-consuming iterations particularly when the dataset scales. In this paper, targeting at the emerging vision transformer (ViT), we present NasHD, a hyperdimensional computing based supervised learning model to rank the performance given the architectures and configurations. Different from other learning based methods, NasHD is faster thanks to the high parallel processing of HDC architecture. We also evaluated two HDC encoding schemes: Gram-based and Record-based of NasHD on their performance and efficiency. On the VIMER-UFO benchmark dataset of 8 applications from a diverse range of domains, NasHD Record can rank the performance of nearly 100K vision transformer models with about 1 minute while still achieving comparable results with sophisticated models.
abstract_zh: 神经架构搜索（NAS）是一种用于深度学习设计自动化的自动化架构工程方法，可替代手动且容易出错的模型开发、选择、评估和性能估计过程。然而，NAS 的一个主要障碍是极其苛刻的计算资源需求和耗时的迭代，特别是当数据集扩展时。在本文中，针对新兴的视觉变换器（ViT），我们提出了 NasHD，一种基于超维计算的监督学习模型，用于对给定架构和配置的性能进行排名。与其他基于学习的方法不同，由于 HDC 架构的高度并行处理，NasHD 速度更快。我们还评估了两种 HDC 编码方案：NasHD 的基于 Gram 和 Record-based 的性能和效率。在包含来自不同领域的 8 个应用程序的 VIMER-UFO 基准数据集上，NasHD Record 可以在大约 1 分钟内对近 100K 视觉变压器模型的性能进行排名，同时仍然获得与复杂模型相当的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出NasHD方法，利用超维计算（HDC）对视觉Transformer（ViT）架构进行高效性能排序，解决传统神经架构搜索（NAS）计算资源消耗大、耗时的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统NAS方法在处理大规模数据集时面临计算资源需求高、迭代耗时长的挑战，尤其针对ViT架构的性能评估效率亟需优化。
AI.method: 基于HDC架构设计监督学习模型NasHD，采用Gram-based和Record-based两种编码方案，利用HDC的高并行性加速架构性能排序过程。
AI.result: 在VIMER-UFO基准数据集上，NasHD Record方案可在约1分钟内完成近10万ViT模型的性能排序，且结果与复杂模型相当。
AI.conclusion: NasHD通过HDC实现高效ViT架构性能评估，为NAS提供了计算效率与精度平衡的可行方案。

authors_text: Justice Mason,Christine Allen-Blanchette,Nicholas Zolman,Elizabeth Davison,Naomi Leonard
title: Learning Interpretable Dynamics from Images of a Freely Rotating 3D Rigid Body
title_zh: 从自由旋转 3D 刚体的图像中学习可解释的动力学
abstract: In many real-world settings, image observations of freely rotating 3D rigid bodies, such as satellites, may be available when low-dimensional measurements are not. However, the high-dimensionality of image data precludes the use of classical estimation techniques to learn the dynamics and a lack of interpretability reduces the usefulness of standard deep learning methods. In this work, we present a physics-informed neural network model to estimate and predict 3D rotational dynamics from image sequences. We achieve this using a multi-stage prediction pipeline that maps individual images to a latent representation homeomorphic to $\mathbf{SO}(3)$, computes angular velocities from latent pairs, and predicts future latent states using the Hamiltonian equations of motion with a learned representation of the Hamiltonian. We demonstrate the efficacy of our approach on a new rotating rigid-body dataset with sequences of rotating cubes and rectangular prisms with uniform and non-uniform density.
summary: In many real-world settings, image observations of freely rotating 3D rigid bodies, such as satellites, may be available when low-dimensional measurements are not. However, the high-dimensionality of image data precludes the use of classical estimation techniques to learn the dynamics and a lack of interpretability reduces the usefulness of standard deep learning methods. In this work, we present a physics-informed neural network model to estimate and predict 3D rotational dynamics from image sequences. We achieve this using a multi-stage prediction pipeline that maps individual images to a latent representation homeomorphic to $\mathbf{SO}(3)$, computes angular velocities from latent pairs, and predicts future latent states using the Hamiltonian equations of motion with a learned representation of the Hamiltonian. We demonstrate the efficacy of our approach on a new rotating rigid-body dataset with sequences of rotating cubes and rectangular prisms with uniform and non-uniform density.
abstract_zh: 在许多现实世界中，当低维测量不可用时，可以对自由旋转的 3D 刚体（例如卫星）进行图像观测。然而，图像数据的高维性阻碍了使用经典估计技术来学习动态，并且缺乏可解释性降低了标准深度学习方法的实用性。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种基于物理的神经网络模型，用于根据图像序列估计和预测 3D 旋转动力学。我们使用多级预测管道来实现这一目标，该管道将单个图像映射到与 $\mathbf{SO}(3)$ 同构的潜在表示，从潜在对计算角速度，并使用哈密顿运动方程和学习的哈密顿量表示来预测未来的潜在状态。我们在一个新的旋转刚体数据集上展示了我们的方法的有效性，该数据集包含具有均匀和非均匀密度的旋转立方体和矩形棱柱序列。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于物理信息的神经网络模型，从自由旋转的3D刚体图像序列中学习可解释的三维旋转动力学。
AI.motivation: 传统估计方法难以处理高维图像数据，而标准深度学习方法缺乏可解释性，无法有效学习自由旋转3D刚体的动力学特性。
AI.method: 采用多阶段预测流水线：将单张图像映射到与SO(3)同胚的潜在表示，通过潜在表示对计算角速度，并利用学习到的哈密顿量的哈密顿运动方程预测未来潜在状态。
AI.result: 在包含均匀和非均匀密度旋转立方体和长方体的新数据集上验证了方法的有效性，成功估计了三维旋转动力学。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过结合物理先验知识与深度学习，实现了从图像中学习可解释的旋转动力学，为缺乏低维测量的场景提供了有效解决方案。

authors_text: Risheek Garrepalli
title: Oracle Analysis of Representations for Deep Open Set Detection
title_zh: Oracle 深度开集检测表示分析
abstract: The problem of detecting a novel class at run time is known as Open Set Detection & is important for various real-world applications like medical application, autonomous driving, etc. Open Set Detection within context of deep learning involves solving two problems: (i) Must map the input images into a latent representation that contains enough information to detect the outliers, and (ii) Must learn an anomaly scoring function that can extract this information from the latent representation to identify the anomalies. Research in deep anomaly detection methods has progressed slowly. One reason may be that most papers simultaneously introduce new representation learning techniques and new anomaly scoring approaches. The goal of this work is to improve this methodology by providing ways of separately measuring the effectiveness of the representation learning and anomaly scoring. This work makes two methodological contributions. The first is to introduce the notion of Oracle anomaly detection for quantifying the information available in a learned latent representation. The second is to introduce Oracle representation learning, which produces a representation that is guaranteed to be sufficient for accurate anomaly detection. These two techniques help researchers to separate the quality of the learned representation from the performance of the anomaly scoring mechanism so that they can debug and improve their systems. The methods also provide an upper limit on how much open category detection can be improved through better anomaly scoring mechanisms. The combination of the two oracles gives an upper limit on the performance that any open category detection method could achieve. This work introduces these two oracle techniques and demonstrates their utility by applying them to several leading open category detection methods.
summary: The problem of detecting a novel class at run time is known as Open Set Detection & is important for various real-world applications like medical application, autonomous driving, etc. Open Set Detection within context of deep learning involves solving two problems: (i) Must map the input images into a latent representation that contains enough information to detect the outliers, and (ii) Must learn an anomaly scoring function that can extract this information from the latent representation to identify the anomalies. Research in deep anomaly detection methods has progressed slowly. One reason may be that most papers simultaneously introduce new representation learning techniques and new anomaly scoring approaches. The goal of this work is to improve this methodology by providing ways of separately measuring the effectiveness of the representation learning and anomaly scoring. This work makes two methodological contributions. The first is to introduce the notion of Oracle anomaly detection for quantifying the information available in a learned latent representation. The second is to introduce Oracle representation learning, which produces a representation that is guaranteed to be sufficient for accurate anomaly detection. These two techniques help researchers to separate the quality of the learned representation from the performance of the anomaly scoring mechanism so that they can debug and improve their systems. The methods also provide an upper limit on how much open category detection can be improved through better anomaly scoring mechanisms. The combination of the two oracles gives an upper limit on the performance that any open category detection method could achieve. This work introduces these two oracle techniques and demonstrates their utility by applying them to several leading open category detection methods.
abstract_zh: 在运行时检测新类的问题被称为开集检测，对于医疗应用、自动驾驶等各种现实应用非常重要。深度学习背景下的开集检测涉及解决两个问题：（i）必须将输入图像映射到包含足够信息来检测异常值的潜在表示，以及（ii）必须学习异常评分函数，该函数可以从潜在表示中提取此信息以识别异常。深度异常检测方法的研究进展缓慢。原因之一可能是大多数论文同时引入了新的表示学习技术和新的异常评分方法。这项工作的目标是通过提供单独测量表示学习和异常评分有效性的方法来改进这种方法。这项工作做出了两个方法论贡献。首先是介绍 Oracle 异常检测的概念，用于量化学习的潜在表示中可用的信息。第二个是引入 Oracle 表示学习，它产生的表示足以保证准确的异常检测。这两种技术帮助研究人员将学习表示的质量与异常评分机制的性能分开，以便他们可以调试和改进他们的系统。这些方法还提供了通过更好的异常评分机制可以改进多少开放类别检测的上限。两个预言机的结合给出了任何开放类别检测方法可以实现的性能上限。这项工作介绍了这两种预言机技术，并通过将它们应用于几种领先的开放类别检测方法来展示它们的实用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出两种Oracle方法分别评估深度开放集检测中的表示学习和异常评分效果，以提升系统性能。
AI.motivation: 现有方法同时改进表示学习和异常评分，难以区分两者贡献，导致开放集检测进展缓慢。
AI.method: 引入Oracle异常检测量化表示信息量，Oracle表示学习生成保证足够检测的表示，分离评估两部分效果。
AI.result: 将两种Oracle方法应用于主流开放集检测模型，验证其有效性并提供性能上限参考。
AI.conclusion: 两种Oracle技术可帮助研究者独立优化表示学习和评分机制，为开放集检测设定理论性能边界。

authors_text: Marcos V. Conde,Ui-Jin Choi,Maxime Burchi,Radu Timofte
title: Swin2SR: SwinV2 Transformer for Compressed Image Super-Resolution and Restoration
title_zh: Swin2SR：用于压缩图像超分辨率和恢复的 SwinV2 Transformer
comment: European Conference on Computer Vision (ECCV 2022) Workshops
comments: European Conference on Computer Vision (ECCV 2022) Workshops
abstract: Compression plays an important role on the efficient transmission and storage of images and videos through band-limited systems such as streaming services, virtual reality or videogames. However, compression unavoidably leads to artifacts and the loss of the original information, which may severely degrade the visual quality. For these reasons, quality enhancement of compressed images has become a popular research topic. While most state-of-the-art image restoration methods are based on convolutional neural networks, other transformers-based methods such as SwinIR, show impressive performance on these tasks. In this paper, we explore the novel Swin Transformer V2, to improve SwinIR for image super-resolution, and in particular, the compressed input scenario. Using this method we can tackle the major issues in training transformer vision models, such as training instability, resolution gaps between pre-training and fine-tuning, and hunger on data. We conduct experiments on three representative tasks: JPEG compression artifacts removal, image super-resolution (classical and lightweight), and compressed image super-resolution. Experimental results demonstrate that our method, Swin2SR, can improve the training convergence and performance of SwinIR, and is a top-5 solution at the "AIM 2022 Challenge on Super-Resolution of Compressed Image and Video".
summary: Compression plays an important role on the efficient transmission and storage of images and videos through band-limited systems such as streaming services, virtual reality or videogames. However, compression unavoidably leads to artifacts and the loss of the original information, which may severely degrade the visual quality. For these reasons, quality enhancement of compressed images has become a popular research topic. While most state-of-the-art image restoration methods are based on convolutional neural networks, other transformers-based methods such as SwinIR, show impressive performance on these tasks. In this paper, we explore the novel Swin Transformer V2, to improve SwinIR for image super-resolution, and in particular, the compressed input scenario. Using this method we can tackle the major issues in training transformer vision models, such as training instability, resolution gaps between pre-training and fine-tuning, and hunger on data. We conduct experiments on three representative tasks: JPEG compression artifacts removal, image super-resolution (classical and lightweight), and compressed image super-resolution. Experimental results demonstrate that our method, Swin2SR, can improve the training convergence and performance of SwinIR, and is a top-5 solution at the "AIM 2022 Challenge on Super-Resolution of Compressed Image and Video".
abstract_zh: 压缩对于通过流媒体服务、虚拟现实或视频游戏等带宽受限系统有效传输和存储图像和视频起着重要作用。然而，压缩不可避免地会导致伪影和原始信息的丢失，这可能会严重降低视觉质量。由于这些原因，压缩图像的质量增强已成为一个热门的研究课题。虽然大多数最先进的图像恢复方法都基于卷积神经网络，但其他基于 Transformer 的方法（例如 SwinIR）在这些任务上表现出了令人印象深刻的性能。在本文中，我们探索了新颖的 Swin Transformer V2，以改进 SwinIR 的图像超分辨率，特别是压缩输入场景。使用这种方法，我们可以解决训练 Transformer 视觉模型时的主要问题，例如训练不稳定、预训练和微调之间的分辨率差距以及数据匮乏。我们对三个代表性任务进行了实验：JPEG 压缩伪影去除、图像超分辨率（经典和轻量级）和压缩图像超分辨率。实验结果表明，我们的方法 Swin2SR 可以提高 SwinIR 的训练收敛性和性能，并且是“AIM 2022 压缩图像和视频超分辨率挑战赛”中的前 5 名解决方案。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于Swin Transformer V2的图像超分辨率和修复方法Swin2SR，用于提升压缩图像的质量。
AI.motivation: 图像压缩在传输和存储中至关重要，但会引入伪影并导致信息丢失。现有的基于卷积神经网络的方法在处理压缩图像时效果有限，而基于Transformer的方法如SwinIR表现优异，但仍有训练不稳定、分辨率差距和数据需求大的问题。
AI.method: 本文采用Swin Transformer V2改进SwinIR，以解决训练不稳定、预训练与微调之间的分辨率差距和数据需求大的问题，并在JPEG压缩伪影去除、图像超分辨率和压缩图像超分辨率三个任务上进行实验。
AI.result: 实验表明，Swin2SR在训练收敛性和性能方面优于SwinIR，并在AIM 2022压缩图像和视频超分辨率挑战赛中位列前五。
AI.conclusion: Swin2SR通过引入Swin Transformer V2有效提升了压缩图像的超分辨率和修复性能，为相关领域提供了新的解决方案。

authors_text: Harsh Kumar,Ilya Musabirov,Jiakai Shi,Adele Lauzon,Kwan Kiu Choy,Ofek Gross,Dana Kulzhabayeva,Joseph Jay Williams
title: Exploring The Design of Prompts For Applying GPT-3 based Chatbots: A Mental Wellbeing Case Study on Mechanical Turk
title_zh: 探索应用基于 GPT-3 的聊天机器人的提示设计：Mechanical Turk 的心理健康案例研究
abstract: Large-Language Models like GPT-3 have the potential to enable HCI designers and researchers to create more human-like and helpful chatbots for specific applications. But evaluating the feasibility of these chatbots and designing prompts that optimize GPT-3 for a specific task is challenging. We present a case study in tackling these questions, applying GPT-3 to a brief 5-minute chatbot that anyone can talk to better manage their mood. We report a randomized factorial experiment with 945 participants on Mechanical Turk that tests three dimensions of prompt design to initialize the chatbot (identity, intent, and behaviour), and present both quantitative and qualitative analyses of conversations and user perceptions of the chatbot. We hope other HCI designers and researchers can build on this case study, for other applications of GPT-3 based chatbots to specific tasks, and build on and extend the methods we use for prompt design, and evaluation of the prompt design.
summary: Large-Language Models like GPT-3 have the potential to enable HCI designers and researchers to create more human-like and helpful chatbots for specific applications. But evaluating the feasibility of these chatbots and designing prompts that optimize GPT-3 for a specific task is challenging. We present a case study in tackling these questions, applying GPT-3 to a brief 5-minute chatbot that anyone can talk to better manage their mood. We report a randomized factorial experiment with 945 participants on Mechanical Turk that tests three dimensions of prompt design to initialize the chatbot (identity, intent, and behaviour), and present both quantitative and qualitative analyses of conversations and user perceptions of the chatbot. We hope other HCI designers and researchers can build on this case study, for other applications of GPT-3 based chatbots to specific tasks, and build on and extend the methods we use for prompt design, and evaluation of the prompt design.
abstract_zh: 像 GPT-3 这样的大语言模型有可能使 HCI 设计者和研究人员能够为特定应用程序创建更人性化且更有用的聊天机器人。但评估这些聊天机器人的可行性并设计针对特定任务优化 GPT-3 的提示具有挑战性。我们提出了一个解决这些问题的案例研究，将 GPT-3 应用于一个简短的 5 分钟聊天机器人，任何人都可以通过交谈来更好地管理自己的情绪。我们在 Mechanical Turk 上报告了一项由 945 名参与者参与的随机析因实验，该实验测试了初始化聊天机器人的提示设计的三个维度（身份、意图和行为），并对聊天机器人的对话和用户感知进行了定量和定性分析。我们希望其他 HCI 设计师和研究人员能够在此案例研究的基础上，将基于 GPT-3 的聊天机器人应用于特定任务，并构建和扩展我们用于提示设计和提示设计评估的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文通过案例研究探索基于GPT-3的聊天机器人提示设计，旨在优化其在心理健康场景中的应用效果。
AI.motivation: 尽管大语言模型（如GPT-3）具有潜力，但如何设计有效提示以优化特定任务（如心理健康支持）的聊天机器人仍面临挑战。
AI.method: 在Mechanical Turk平台上开展随机化因子实验，测试身份、意图和行为三个提示维度，结合定量与定性分析评估用户交互和感知。
AI.result: 实验发现不同提示设计显著影响用户对聊天机器人的感知和互动效果，为后续优化提供依据。
AI.conclusion: 本研究为HCI设计者提供了可复用的提示设计框架与评估方法，助力GPT-3在特定任务中的应用扩展。

search_text: Exploring The Design of Prompts For Applying GPT-3 based Chatbots: A Mental Wellbeing Case Study on Mechanical Turk 探索应用基于 GPT-3 的聊天机器人的提示设计：Mechanical Turk 的心理健康案例研究 Large-Language Models like GPT-3 have the potential to enable HCI designers and researchers to create more human-like and helpful chatbots for specific applications. But evaluating the feasibility of these chatbots and designing prompts that optimize GPT-3 for a specific task is challenging. We present a case study in tackling these questions, applying GPT-3 to a brief 5-minute chatbot that anyone can talk to better manage their mood. We report a randomized factorial experiment with 945 participants on Mechanical Turk that tests three dimensions of prompt design to initialize the chatbot (identity, intent, and behaviour), and present both quantitative and qualitative analyses of conversations and user perceptions of the chatbot. We hope other HCI designers and researchers can build on this case study, for other applications of GPT-3 based chatbots to specific tasks, and build on and extend the methods we use for prompt design, and evaluation of the prompt design. 像 GPT-3 这样的大语言模型有可能使 HCI 设计者和研究人员能够为特定应用程序创建更人性化且更有用的聊天机器人。但评估这些聊天机器人的可行性并设计针对特定任务优化 GPT-3 的提示具有挑战性。我们提出了一个解决这些问题的案例研究，将 GPT-3 应用于一个简短的 5 分钟聊天机器人，任何人都可以通过交谈来更好地管理自己的情绪。我们在 Mechanical Turk 上报告了一项由 945 名参与者参与的随机析因实验，该实验测试了初始化聊天机器人的提示设计的三个维度（身份、意图和行为），并对聊天机器人的对话和用户感知进行了定量和定性分析。我们希望其他 HCI 设计师和研究人员能够在此案例研究的基础上，将基于 GPT-3 的聊天机器人应用于特定任务，并构建和扩展我们用于提示设计和提示设计评估的方法。 本文通过案例研究探索基于GPT-3的聊天机器人提示设计，旨在优化其在心理健康场景中的应用效果。 Harsh Kumar,Ilya Musabirov,Jiakai Shi,Adele Lauzon,Kwan Kiu Choy,Ofek Gross,Dana Kulzhabayeva,Joseph Jay Williams cs.HC,cs.CY

authors_text: Emmanuel Martinez,Edwin Vargas,Henry Arguello
title: Fast Disparity Estimation from a Single Compressed Light Field Measurement
title_zh: 通过单次压缩光场测量快速估计视差
abstract: The abundant spatial and angular information from light fields has allowed the development of multiple disparity estimation approaches. However, the acquisition of light fields requires high storage and processing cost, limiting the use of this technology in practical applications. To overcome these drawbacks, the compressive sensing (CS) theory has allowed the development of optical architectures to acquire a single coded light field measurement. This measurement is decoded using an optimization algorithm or deep neural network that requires high computational costs. The traditional approach for disparity estimation from compressed light fields requires first recovering the entire light field and then a post-processing step, thus requiring long times. In contrast, this work proposes a fast disparity estimation from a single compressed measurement by omitting the recovery step required in traditional approaches. Specifically, we propose to jointly optimize an optical architecture for acquiring a single coded light field snapshot and a convolutional neural network (CNN) for estimating the disparity maps. Experimentally, the proposed method estimates disparity maps comparable with those obtained from light fields reconstructed using deep learning approaches. Furthermore, the proposed method is 20 times faster in training and inference than the best method that estimates the disparity from reconstructed light fields.
summary: The abundant spatial and angular information from light fields has allowed the development of multiple disparity estimation approaches. However, the acquisition of light fields requires high storage and processing cost, limiting the use of this technology in practical applications. To overcome these drawbacks, the compressive sensing (CS) theory has allowed the development of optical architectures to acquire a single coded light field measurement. This measurement is decoded using an optimization algorithm or deep neural network that requires high computational costs. The traditional approach for disparity estimation from compressed light fields requires first recovering the entire light field and then a post-processing step, thus requiring long times. In contrast, this work proposes a fast disparity estimation from a single compressed measurement by omitting the recovery step required in traditional approaches. Specifically, we propose to jointly optimize an optical architecture for acquiring a single coded light field snapshot and a convolutional neural network (CNN) for estimating the disparity maps. Experimentally, the proposed method estimates disparity maps comparable with those obtained from light fields reconstructed using deep learning approaches. Furthermore, the proposed method is 20 times faster in training and inference than the best method that estimates the disparity from reconstructed light fields.
abstract_zh: 来自光场的丰富的空间和角度信息使得多种视差估计方法的发展成为可能。然而，光场的获取需要较高的存储和处理成本，限制了该技术在实际应用中的使用。为了克服这些缺点，压缩传感（CS）理论已经允许光学架构的发展来获取单个编码光场测量。该测量结果使用优化算法或深度神经网络进行解码，需要很高的计算成本。传统的压缩光场视差估计方法需要首先恢复整个光场，然后进行后处理步骤，因此需要很长时间。相比之下，这项工作提出了通过省略传统方法中所需的恢复步骤，从单个压缩测量中快速估计视差。具体来说，我们建议联合优化用于获取单个编码光场快照的光学架构和用于估计视差图的卷积神经网络（CNN）。在实验上，所提出的方法估计的视差图与使用深度学习方法重建的光场获得的视差图相当。此外，该方法的训练和推理速度比估计重建光场视差的最佳方法快 20 倍。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需完整光场恢复的快速视差估计方法，通过联合优化光学架构与卷积神经网络直接处理压缩光场测量数据。
AI.motivation: 传统光场视差估计需先进行耗时的光场恢复，且光场采集存在高存储与计算成本，限制了实际应用。
AI.method: 设计单次编码光场采集光学系统，结合卷积神经网络直接从压缩测量数据中联合优化视差估计，跳过光场恢复步骤。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法生成的视差图质量与深度学习重建方法相当，且训练和推理速度提升20倍。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法在保证视差估计精度的同时显著降低计算成本，为压缩光场的实际应用提供了高效解决方案。

authors_text: Mohamed Amine Kerkouri,Marouane Tliba,Aladine Chetouani,Alessandro Bruno
title: A domain adaptive deep learning solution for scanpath prediction of paintings
title_zh: 用于绘画扫描路径预测的领域自适应深度学习解决方案
abstract: Cultural heritage understanding and preservation is an important issue for society as it represents a fundamental aspect of its identity. Paintings represent a significant part of cultural heritage, and are the subject of study continuously. However, the way viewers perceive paintings is strictly related to the so-called HVS (Human Vision System) behaviour. This paper focuses on the eye-movement analysis of viewers during the visual experience of a certain number of paintings. In further details, we introduce a new approach to predicting human visual attention, which impacts several cognitive functions for humans, including the fundamental understanding of a scene, and then extend it to painting images. The proposed new architecture ingests images and returns scanpaths, a sequence of points featuring a high likelihood of catching viewers' attention. We use an FCNN (Fully Convolutional Neural Network), in which we exploit a differentiable channel-wise selection and Soft-Argmax modules. We also incorporate learnable Gaussian distributions onto the network bottleneck to simulate visual attention process bias in natural scene images. Furthermore, to reduce the effect of shifts between different domains (i.e. natural images, painting), we urge the model to learn unsupervised general features from other domains using a gradient reversal classifier. The results obtained by our model outperform existing state-of-the-art ones in terms of accuracy and efficiency.
summary: Cultural heritage understanding and preservation is an important issue for society as it represents a fundamental aspect of its identity. Paintings represent a significant part of cultural heritage, and are the subject of study continuously. However, the way viewers perceive paintings is strictly related to the so-called HVS (Human Vision System) behaviour. This paper focuses on the eye-movement analysis of viewers during the visual experience of a certain number of paintings. In further details, we introduce a new approach to predicting human visual attention, which impacts several cognitive functions for humans, including the fundamental understanding of a scene, and then extend it to painting images. The proposed new architecture ingests images and returns scanpaths, a sequence of points featuring a high likelihood of catching viewers' attention. We use an FCNN (Fully Convolutional Neural Network), in which we exploit a differentiable channel-wise selection and Soft-Argmax modules. We also incorporate learnable Gaussian distributions onto the network bottleneck to simulate visual attention process bias in natural scene images. Furthermore, to reduce the effect of shifts between different domains (i.e. natural images, painting), we urge the model to learn unsupervised general features from other domains using a gradient reversal classifier. The results obtained by our model outperform existing state-of-the-art ones in terms of accuracy and efficiency.
abstract_zh: 文化遗产的理解和保护是社会的一个重要问题，因为它代表了其身份的一个基本方面。绘画代表了文化遗产的重要组成部分，并且一直是研究的主题。然而，观众感知绘画的方式与所谓的 HVS（人类视觉系统）行为密切相关。本文重点研究了观看者在观看一定数量的画作的视觉体验过程中的眼动分析。更详细地说，我们介绍了一种预测人类视觉注意力的新方法，它会影响人类的多种认知功能，包括对场景的基本理解，然后将其扩展到绘画图像。所提出的新架构摄取图像并返回扫描路径，这是一系列很可能吸引观众注意力的点。我们使用 FCNN（全卷积神经网络），其中我们利用可微分通道选择和 Soft-Argmax 模块。我们还将可学习的高斯分布合并到网络瓶颈中，以模拟自然场景图像中的视觉注意过程偏差。此外，为了减少不同领域（即自然图像、绘画）之间变化的影响，我们敦促模型使用梯度反转分类器从其他领域学习无监督的一般特征。我们的模型获得的结果在准确性和效率方面优于现有的最先进的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种领域自适应深度学习方法，用于预测绘画作品的扫视路径。
AI.motivation: 绘画作为文化遗产的重要组成部分，其观众视觉感知研究对文化保护具有重要意义。现有方法在跨领域（自然图像与绘画）应用中存在性能差异，需改进视觉注意力预测模型。
AI.method: 基于全卷积神经网络（FCNN），结合可微通道选择、Soft-Argmax模块及可学习高斯分布模拟视觉注意偏差，并引入梯度反转分类器实现跨领域无监督特征学习。
AI.result: 模型在扫视路径预测任务中超越现有最先进方法，在准确率和效率指标上表现更优。
AI.conclusion: 通过领域自适应技术，本文方法有效提升了绘画扫视路径预测性能，为文化遗产研究提供新的技术手段。

search_text: A domain adaptive deep learning solution for scanpath prediction of paintings 用于绘画扫描路径预测的领域自适应深度学习解决方案 Cultural heritage understanding and preservation is an important issue for society as it represents a fundamental aspect of its identity. Paintings represent a significant part of cultural heritage, and are the subject of study continuously. However, the way viewers perceive paintings is strictly related to the so-called HVS (Human Vision System) behaviour. This paper focuses on the eye-movement analysis of viewers during the visual experience of a certain number of paintings. In further details, we introduce a new approach to predicting human visual attention, which impacts several cognitive functions for humans, including the fundamental understanding of a scene, and then extend it to painting images. The proposed new architecture ingests images and returns scanpaths, a sequence of points featuring a high likelihood of catching viewers' attention. We use an FCNN (Fully Convolutional Neural Network), in which we exploit a differentiable channel-wise selection and Soft-Argmax modules. We also incorporate learnable Gaussian distributions onto the network bottleneck to simulate visual attention process bias in natural scene images. Furthermore, to reduce the effect of shifts between different domains (i.e. natural images, painting), we urge the model to learn unsupervised general features from other domains using a gradient reversal classifier. The results obtained by our model outperform existing state-of-the-art ones in terms of accuracy and efficiency. 文化遗产的理解和保护是社会的一个重要问题，因为它代表了其身份的一个基本方面。绘画代表了文化遗产的重要组成部分，并且一直是研究的主题。然而，观众感知绘画的方式与所谓的 HVS（人类视觉系统）行为密切相关。本文重点研究了观看者在观看一定数量的画作的视觉体验过程中的眼动分析。更详细地说，我们介绍了一种预测人类视觉注意力的新方法，它会影响人类的多种认知功能，包括对场景的基本理解，然后将其扩展到绘画图像。所提出的新架构摄取图像并返回扫描路径，这是一系列很可能吸引观众注意力的点。我们使用 FCNN（全卷积神经网络），其中我们利用可微分通道选择和 Soft-Argmax 模块。我们还将可学习的高斯分布合并到网络瓶颈中，以模拟自然场景图像中的视觉注意过程偏差。此外，为了减少不同领域（即自然图像、绘画）之间变化的影响，我们敦促模型使用梯度反转分类器从其他领域学习无监督的一般特征。我们的模型获得的结果在准确性和效率方面优于现有的最先进的结果。 本文提出一种领域自适应深度学习方法，用于预测绘画作品的扫视路径。 Mohamed Amine Kerkouri,Marouane Tliba,Aladine Chetouani,Alessandro Bruno cs.CV

authors_text: Anbang Yang,Mahya Beheshti,Todd E Hudson,Rajesh Vedanthan,Wachara Riewpaiboon,Pattanasak Mongkolwat,Chen Feng,John-Ross Rizzo
title: UNav: An Infrastructure-Independent Vision-Based Navigation System for People with Blindness and Low vision
title_zh: UNV：为盲人和低视力人士提供独立于基础设施的视觉导航系统
abstract: Vision-based localization approaches now underpin newly emerging navigation pipelines for myriad use cases from robotics to assistive technologies. Compared to sensor-based solutions, vision-based localization does not require pre-installed sensor infrastructure, which is costly, time-consuming, and/or often infeasible at scale. Herein, we propose a novel vision-based localization pipeline for a specific use case: navigation support for end-users with blindness and low vision. Given a query image taken by an end-user on a mobile application, the pipeline leverages a visual place recognition (VPR) algorithm to find similar images in a reference image database of the target space. The geolocations of these similar images are utilized in downstream tasks that employ a weighted-average method to estimate the end-user's location and a perspective-n-point (PnP) algorithm to estimate the end-user's direction. Additionally, this system implements Dijkstra's algorithm to calculate a shortest path based on a navigable map that includes trip origin and destination. The topometric map used for localization and navigation is built using a customized graphical user interface that projects a 3D reconstructed sparse map, built from a sequence of images, to the corresponding a priori 2D floor plan. Sequential images used for map construction can be collected in a pre-mapping step or scavenged through public databases/citizen science. The end-to-end system can be installed on any internet-accessible device with a camera that hosts a custom mobile application. For evaluation purposes, mapping and localization were tested in a complex hospital environment. The evaluation results demonstrate that our system can achieve localization with an average error of less than 1 meter without knowledge of the camera's intrinsic parameters, such as focal length.

Compared to sensor-based solutions, vision-based localization does not require pre-installed sensor infrastructure, which is costly, time-consuming, and/or often infeasible at scale. Herein, we propose a novel vision-based localization pipeline for a specific use case: navigation support for end-users with blindness and low vision. The geolocations of these similar images are utilized in downstream tasks that employ a weighted-average method to estimate the end-user's location and a perspective-n-point (PnP) algorithm to estimate the end-user's direction. Additionally, this system implements Dijkstra's algorithm to calculate a shortest path based on a navigable map that includes trip origin and destination. The topometric map used for localization and navigation is built using a customized graphical user interface that projects a 3D reconstructed sparse map, built from a sequence of images, to the corresponding a priori 2D floor plan. Sequential images used for map construction can be collected in a pre-mapping step or scavenged through public databases/citizen science. The end-to-end system can be installed on any internet-accessible device with a camera that hosts a custom mobile application. For evaluation purposes, mapping and localization were tested in a complex hospital environment. The evaluation results demonstrate that our system can achieve localization with an average error of less than 1 meter without knowledge of the camera's intrinsic parameters, such as focal length.
abstract_zh: 基于视觉的定位方法现在支撑着从机器人到辅助技术的无数用例的新兴导航管道。与基于传感器的解决方案相比，基于视觉的定位不需要预先安装传感器基础设施，而这种基础设施成本高昂、耗时且/或通常无法大规模实施。在此，我们针对特定用例提出了一种新颖的基于视觉的本地化管道：为失明和弱视的最终用户提供导航支持。给定最终用户在移动应用程序上拍摄的查询图像，管道利用视觉位置识别 (VPR) 算法在目标空间的参考图像数据库中查找相似图像。这些相似图像的地理位置用于下游任务，这些任务采用加权平均方法来估计最终用户的位置，并使用透视 n 点 (PnP) 算法来估计最终用户的方向。此外，该系统还实施 Dijkstra 算法，根据包括行程起点和目的地的可导航地图计算最短路径。用于定位和导航的地形图是使用定制的图形用户界面构建的，该界面将根据图像序列构建的 3D 重建稀疏地图投影到相应的先验 2D 平面图。用于地图构建的序列图像可以在预制图步骤中收集，或者通过公共数据库/公民科学进行清理。该端到端系统可以安装在任何可通过互联网访问且带有摄像头并托管自定义移动应用程序的设备上。出于评估目的，在复杂的医院环境中测试了地图绘制和定位。评估结果表明，我们的系统可以在不知道相机焦距等内在参数的情况下实现平均误差小于1米的定位。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需基础设施的视觉导航系统UNav，用于帮助盲人和低视力人群进行定位与导航。
AI.motivation: 传统传感器导航方案依赖昂贵且难以大规模部署的基础设施，而视觉定位方法无需此类设施，更适合为视障人士提供低成本、可扩展的导航支持。
AI.method: 系统通过视觉地点识别（VPR）算法匹配查询图像与参考图像数据库，结合加权平均法和PnP算法估算位置与方向，利用Dijkstra算法计算路径。地图通过3D稀疏重建投影至2D平面图构建，图像数据可来自预采集或公共数据库。
AI.result: 在复杂医院环境中测试显示，系统在无需相机内参的情况下实现平均定位误差小于1米。
AI.conclusion: UNav验证了基于视觉的无基础设施导航系统在视障辅助领域的有效性，为实际应用提供了可行方案。

authors_text: Thomas Dubail,Fidel Alejandro Guerrero Peña,Heitor Rapela Medeiros,Masih Aminbeidokhti,Eric Granger,Marco Pedersoli
title: Privacy-Preserving Person Detection Using Low-Resolution Infrared Cameras
title_zh: 使用低分辨率红外摄像机进行隐私保护人员检测
abstract: In intelligent building management, knowing the number of people and their location in a room are important for better control of its illumination, ventilation, and heating with reduced costs and improved comfort. This is typically achieved by detecting people using compact embedded devices that are installed on the room's ceiling, and that integrate low-resolution infrared camera, which conceals each person's identity. However, for accurate detection, state-of-the-art deep learning models still require supervised training using a large annotated dataset of images. In this paper, we investigate cost-effective methods that are suitable for person detection based on low-resolution infrared images. Results indicate that for such images, we can reduce the amount of supervision and computation, while still achieving a high level of detection accuracy. Going from single-shot detectors that require bounding box annotations of each person in an image, to auto-encoders that only rely on unlabelled images that do not contain people, allows for considerable savings in terms of annotation costs, and for models with lower computational costs. We validate these experimental findings on two challenging top-view datasets with low-resolution infrared images.
summary: In intelligent building management, knowing the number of people and their location in a room are important for better control of its illumination, ventilation, and heating with reduced costs and improved comfort. This is typically achieved by detecting people using compact embedded devices that are installed on the room's ceiling, and that integrate low-resolution infrared camera, which conceals each person's identity. However, for accurate detection, state-of-the-art deep learning models still require supervised training using a large annotated dataset of images. In this paper, we investigate cost-effective methods that are suitable for person detection based on low-resolution infrared images. Results indicate that for such images, we can reduce the amount of supervision and computation, while still achieving a high level of detection accuracy. Going from single-shot detectors that require bounding box annotations of each person in an image, to auto-encoders that only rely on unlabelled images that do not contain people, allows for considerable savings in terms of annotation costs, and for models with lower computational costs. We validate these experimental findings on two challenging top-view datasets with low-resolution infrared images.
abstract_zh: 在智能建筑管理中，了解房间内的人数及其位置对于更好地控制照明、通风和供暖、降低成本和提高舒适度非常重要。这通常是通过使用安装在房间天花板上的紧凑型嵌入式设备来检测人员来实现的，这些设备集成了低分辨率红外摄像机，可以隐藏每个人的身份。然而，为了准确检测，最先进的深度学习模型仍然需要使用大量带注释的图像数据集进行监督训练。在本文中，我们研究了适合基于低分辨率红外图像的人员检测的经济有效的方法。结果表明，对于此类图像，我们可以减少监督和计算量，同时仍然实现高水平的检测精度。从需要图像中每个人的边界框注释的单次检测器，到仅依赖于不包含人物的未标记图像的自动编码器，可以在注释成本和计算成本较低的模型方面节省大量成本。我们在两个具有低分辨率红外图像的具有挑战性的顶视图数据集上验证了这些实验结果。

AI.tldr: 本文研究利用低分辨率红外摄像头进行隐私保护的人体检测方法。
AI.motivation: 传统人体检测需依赖大量标注数据，成本高昂且可能侵犯隐私，需寻找更经济且隐私友好的解决方案。
AI.method: 从需标注边界框的单次检测器转向仅依赖无标签图像的自编码器，降低标注与计算成本。
AI.result: 在两个低分辨率红外数据集上验证，方法显著减少标注成本与计算量，同时保持高检测精度。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为智能建筑管理中的隐私保护人体检测提供了高效低成本的可行方案。

ai_content.conclusion: 该方法为智能建筑管理中的隐私保护人体检测提供了高效低成本的可行方案。
